14: Chick and his best friend Fox live in the little town of Hensville.
One day Professor Pheasant sends a secret letter to Chick.
The letter leads to the Golden Island deep in the Great Forest.
Chick asks Fox and Owl and Hare to come along on the trip.
The whole group meets at the Great Oak at the edge of Hensville.
The adventure of Chick and his friends in the Great Forest begins.
Chick wants to climb the High Mountain in the North.
His friend Fox knows the path to the Top of the High Mountain.
Along the way they pass the Village of Master Badger and his family.
Master Badger gives Chick and Fox a warm meal for the road.
At the Great Lake they rest at the inn called The Feathered Hat.
At last Chick and Fox reach the very Top of the High Mountain.
Chick and his helper Rex are building something big in the Workshop.
Rex is a robot made by Professor Pheasant and very very smart.
Rex can walk and talk and even cook for Chick and Fox.
The whole neighbourhood of Hensville comes to see Rex and Chick.
Professor Pheasant is very proud of the great work done by Chick.
Rex and Chick and Fox and Professor Pheasant celebrate with a big feast.
15: Chick packs his map, his compass, his torch, his rope and his lantern.
The forest is dark, wet, cold, silent and a little bit scary.
Chick spots an owl, a fox, a badger, a hare and a hedgehog.
He eats an apple, a pear, a banana, a nut and a biscuit.
The backpack is big, heavy, colourful, full and still very useful.
He reaches camp tired, wet, cold, happy and very proud of himself.
At the market there are apples, pears, plums, cherries and strawberries.
Chick buys bread, cheese, butter, honey and a big piece of cake.
The trader has red, yellow, green, blue and purple ribbons.
Chick tastes the jam, the soup, the cake, the cheese and the pastry.
He buys flowers for his mum, his gran, his aunt, his neighbour and his teacher.
Happy, full, satisfied, tired and content Chick walks back home.
Chick gets out flour, eggs, butter, sugar, milk and vanilla.
He mixes everything slowly, carefully, gently, thoroughly and well.
The cake has strawberries, bananas, cherries, blueberries and raisins on top.
Chick waits, waits, waits, waits and waits one more time.
The cake is sweet, warm, golden, fluffy and absolutely delicious.
Chick shares it with friends, neighbours, his teacher, the postman and his hero.
16: What is that? Did you hear that? Chick jumps! Help!
Is it a monster? A dragon? A giant? Chick trembles!
Run! Hide! Get away! But where?
Chick leaps up! He shouts really loud! The bushes shake!
Aha! Look at that! It is just a tiny hedgehog!
What a fright! What a surprise! What a funny little creature!
Who types the fastest? You? Chick? Or Fox?
Are you ready? Are you sure? Then off we go! Start!
Chick types fast! You type fast too! Who is the quickest?
Faster! Harder! Do not stop! Keep going!
Who wins? You? Chick? Or is it a tie?
It is a tie! Hooray! You both win! Amazing!
Where is the hat? Gone! Vanished! Where did it go?
Is it flying left? Is it flying right? Or is it going up?
Grab it! Chase it! Run after it! Go!
Chick jumps high! Higher! Even higher! He nearly reaches it!
Yes! Got it! The hat is saved! Hooray!
Chick laughs! Fox laughs! Everyone laughs! What a day!
17: Chick has a plan; Fox has an idea; Hare knows the way.
He needs everything: a map; a spade; a compass; a lantern.
Step one: dig; step two: search; step three: cheer.
Chick digs here; Fox digs there; Hare also digs; nobody gives up.
He finds something sparkling: a coin; he shouts: amazing!
His plan: a success; his treasure: found; his feeling: wonderful.
Chick writes a letter; he thinks a long time; then he starts.
Subject: a big party; date: Saturday; location: the forest.
His guests: the fox; the hare; the owl; the badger; the squirrel.
The fox writes back; the hare calls; the owl sends a card; the badger drops by.
Chick notes everything: balloons; cake; games; streamers; music.
Everything is ready: the table; the chairs; the streamers; the cake.
His recipe has four steps: chop; fry; boil; taste.
The recipe says: first the onions; then the carrots; then the potatoes.
Chick adds: celery; parsley; salt; pepper; a bay leaf.
The soup bubbles softly; Chick stirs; he tastes; he nods happily.
His verdict: perfect; his face: beaming; his soup: the very best.
Chick smiles; his recipe is secret; his soup: the finest; his day: perfect.
18: Chick shouts: "I've found something!" Fox asks: "What's it then?"
"What is it?" asks Fox. "Tell us quick!" shouts Hare.
"A magic pen," whispers Chick, "it makes everything you write come true."
It's a wonder! There is a real cake! "Unbelievable!" cries Fox.
Chick writes: 'party' and 'friends'; Fox cheers: "Amazing! It's working!"
Every morning Chick opens his diary and writes: "Today I'm going on an adventure."
"First to the forest," he writes, "then to the lake and the old castle."
In the forest he finds a note: "It's dangerous here! Turn back!"
Chick writes: "I'm going anyway; adventure's waiting for me."
That evening he writes: "It's been the best day of my whole life."
The diary whispers: "These are your most wonderful memories; you're so brave."
Chick sees a sign: 'The House of Words'; he shouts: "Wow!"
"What a place," whispers Chick, "it's so magical and special!"
"This book," says the librarian, "is called 'The Golden Feather'; it's wonderful."
Chick reads aloud: "It's the day the Golden Feather fell; it's a magical tale."
"This is the best book ever," says Chick; "it's as if I'm right there."
It's so quiet in the library; Chick whispers: "I'll come back every single day."
